title: Applying for a job check: process steps :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Applying for a job check: process steps
If you are approved to hire migrants on an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV), including seasonal AEWVs (GWSV and PSV) from 8 December 2025, you must apply for a job check. You may need to advertise the job, engage with Work and Income, or get an endorsement before you apply.

## Who must apply for a job check
As an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) accredited employer, you need to apply for a job check if you are hiring workers on a regular AEWV, or seasonal AEWV — a Global Workforce Seasonal Visa (GWSV) or Peak Seasonal Visa (PSV).
What you need to do before applying, and the evidence you must provide for a job check depends on the visa type.

## How to apply for a job check
- 1 Step 1: Check if you need to advertise the job You must advertise the job unless it: is on the Green List and meets the Green List requirements, or pays at least NZD $70.00 an hour, or is on the Global Workforce Seasonal Visa (GWSV) jobs list. Green list roles — jobs we need people for in New Zealand Global Workforce Seasonal Visa jobs You do not need to advertise the job if you are applying for a job check after a business sale or restructure.

### Step 1: Check if you need to advertise the job
You must advertise the job unless it:
- is on the Green List and meets the Green List requirements, or
- pays at least NZD $70.00 an hour, or
- is on the Global Workforce Seasonal Visa (GWSV) jobs list.
Green list roles — jobs we need people for in New Zealand
Global Workforce Seasonal Visa jobs
You do not need to advertise the job if you are applying for a job check after a business sale or restructure.
- 2 Step 2: Advertise the job When you advertise, you must make genuine attempts to attract and recruit New Zealand workers. You can use a recruitment agency to advertise and find suitable applicants. Your job advertisement needs to include: job title and location a job description with details of the key tasks, duties and responsibilities the minimum and maximum rate of pay or salary, or estimated actual earnings if actual earnings are not guaranteed type of work and minimum guaranteed hours of work the minimum skills, experience and qualifications for the job. Find out how to advertise the job: Advertising the job before you apply for a job check How long you will need to advertise for depends on what skill level the job is the Australian and New Zealand Standard Classification of Occupations (ANZSCO) or the National Occupation List (NOL). The NOL will eventually replace the ANZSCO. If the job is ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3, you must advertise for at least 14 days. If the job is ANZSCO skill level 4 or 5, you must advertise for at least 21 days. How to find your job's skill level

### Step 2: Advertise the job
When you advertise, you must make genuine attempts to attract and recruit New Zealand workers.
You can use a recruitment agency to advertise and find suitable applicants.
Your job advertisement needs to include:
- job title and location
- a job description with details of the key tasks, duties and responsibilities
- the minimum and maximum rate of pay or salary, or estimated actual earnings if actual earnings are not guaranteed
- type of work and minimum guaranteed hours of work
- the minimum skills, experience and qualifications for the job.
Find out how to advertise the job:
Advertising the job before you apply for a job check
How long you will need to advertise for depends on what skill level the job is the Australian and New Zealand Standard Classification of Occupations (ANZSCO) or the National Occupation List (NOL). The NOL will eventually replace the ANZSCO.
- If the job is ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3, you must advertise for at least 14 days.
- If the job is ANZSCO skill level 4 or 5, you must advertise for at least 21 days.
How to find your job's skill level
- 3 Step 3: Engage with Work and Income If the job is ANZSCO skill level 4 or 5 you must engage in with Work and Income to see if they can find someone in New Zealand to do the job. You must do this before you apply for a job check. You must engage with Work and Income in addition to advertising the job. You can advertise the job at the same time. Work and Income will decide either: to list the job, or that they cannot fill the vacancy and you do not need to engage with them further. If you are hiring workers on a Peak Seasonal Visa (PSV), you can either engage with Work and Income to see if they can fill the vacancy, or get an endorsement letter from them. For more information: Getting Work and Income endorsement for a Peak Seasonal Visa Interviewing candidates If Work and Income does refer candidates who you think could be suitable, you must interview them. In most cases, we will accept your declaration that you have engaged with Work and Income in good faith. However, in some instances we may ask to see evidence of that engagement during the job check process. This evidence could include: a copy of the completed Work and Income form, including the date it was submitted, details of your business, title of the job available, the number of positions available, and ANZSCO code if the vacancy is listed by Work and Income, a screenshot of the listing communications with Work and Income, such as emails provided when the listing closed or if they advised they have no job seekers available. We may also contact Work and Income at any time to check you have engaged in good faith. Engaging with Work and Income before you apply for a job check Getting Work and Income endorsement for a Peak Seasonal Visa (PSV) An endorsement is an agreement between your business and Work and Income to fulfill your need for workers by hiring migrants, as well as providing job opportunities for New Zealand job seekers. If you cannot get Work and Income endorsement, you must engage with Work and Income for every job you recruit for, as well as advertise the job. You can advertise the job at the same time as engaging with Work and Income. To get an endorsement from Work and Income: contact Work and Income about your recruitment needs — they may offer endorsement to you when you engage with them agree on the endorsement terms and conditions with Work and Income, for example, the number of jobs reserved for New Zealand job seekers receive an official endorsement agreement letter from Work and Income. The letter will include: your name the endorsement period the occupations and number of jobs the number of jobs you have reserved for New Zealand job seekers the location/region of the job. If you are hiring workers on a PSV and have Work and Income endorsement, you may not need to check they have a New Zealand job seeker to do the job – check the terms and conditions of your endorsement agreement. You must provide us with the Work and Income endorsement letter when you apply for a job check. For more information on PSV endorsement, check the Work and Income website: Hiring migrant workers for peak seasonal roles – Work and Income

### Step 3: Engage with Work and Income
If the job is ANZSCO skill level 4 or 5 you must engage in with Work and Income to see if they can find someone in New Zealand to do the job. You must do this before you apply for a job check.
You must engage with Work and Income in addition to advertising the job. You can advertise the job at the same time.
Work and Income will decide either:
- that they cannot fill the vacancy and you do not need to engage with them further.
If you are hiring workers on a Peak Seasonal Visa (PSV), you can either engage with Work and Income to see if they can fill the vacancy, or get an endorsement letter from them.
For more information:
Getting Work and Income endorsement for a Peak Seasonal Visa

#### Interviewing candidates
If Work and Income does refer candidates who you think could be suitable, you must interview them.
In most cases, we will accept your declaration that you have engaged with Work and Income in good faith. However, in some instances we may ask to see evidence of that engagement during the job check process. This evidence could include:
- a copy of the completed Work and Income form, including the date it was submitted, details of your business, title of the job available, the number of positions available, and ANZSCO code
- if the vacancy is listed by Work and Income, a screenshot of the listing
- communications with Work and Income, such as emails provided when the listing closed or if they advised they have no job seekers available.
We may also contact Work and Income at any time to check you have engaged in good faith.
Engaging with Work and Income before you apply for a job check

#### Getting Work and Income endorsement for a Peak Seasonal Visa (PSV)
An endorsement is an agreement between your business and Work and Income to fulfill your need for workers by hiring migrants, as well as providing job opportunities for New Zealand job seekers.
If you cannot get Work and Income endorsement, you must engage with Work and Income for every job you recruit for, as well as advertise the job. You can advertise the job at the same time as engaging with Work and Income.
To get an endorsement from Work and Income:
- contact Work and Income about your recruitment needs — they may offer endorsement to you when you engage with them
- agree on the endorsement terms and conditions with Work and Income, for example, the number of jobs reserved for New Zealand job seekers
- receive an official endorsement agreement letter from Work and Income.
The letter will include:
- the endorsement period
- the occupations and number of jobs
- the number of jobs you have reserved for New Zealand job seekers
- the location/region of the job.
If you are hiring workers on a PSV and have Work and Income endorsement, you may not need to check they have a New Zealand job seeker to do the job – check the terms and conditions of your endorsement agreement. You must provide us with the Work and Income endorsement letter when you apply for a job check.
For more information on PSV endorsement, check the Work and Income website:
Hiring migrant workers for peak seasonal roles – Work and Income
- 4 Step 4: Check you are ready to apply Before you apply for a job check, check that you: know the visa you want to hire a worker on have the right level of accreditation type for the job and how many people you want to hire know the job title, location and the ANZSCO or NOL code and skill level have advertised the job and engaged with Work and Income if you need to have an endorsement from Work and Income if you are hiring workers on a Peak Seasonal Visa and you have chosen endorsement instead of engaging with Work and Income have prepared your documents. Check if you can apply for AEWV employer accreditation and what type If you advertised the job, make sure you apply for a job check within 90 days of your job advertisement closing. Use our checklist to make sure you have everything you need for your application: Applying for a job check: employer checklist Note When you apply for a job check, we make sure you advertised the job, unless you are exempt. Jobs that are exempt from advertising

### Step 4: Check you are ready to apply
Before you apply for a job check, check that you:
- know the visa you want to hire a worker on
- have the right level of accreditation type for the job and how many people you want to hire
- know the job title, location and the ANZSCO or NOL code and skill level
- have advertised the job and engaged with Work and Income if you need to
- have an endorsement from Work and Income if you are hiring workers on a Peak Seasonal Visa and you have chosen endorsement instead of engaging with Work and Income
- have prepared your documents.
Check if you can apply for AEWV employer accreditation and what type
If you advertised the job, make sure you apply for a job check within 90 days of your job advertisement closing.
Use our checklist to make sure you have everything you need for your application:
Applying for a job check: employer checklist
When you apply for a job check, we make sure you advertised the job, unless you are exempt.
Jobs that are exempt from advertising
- 5 Step 5: Apply online If you have everything you need to apply for a job check, start your application. 1. Log into Immigration Online using RealMe® Log in using RealMe® 2. Go to the 'Employ migrants' tab or choose the 'Employ migrants' button. 3. Go to the 'My granted accreditations' section and find the organisation in the list. 4. In the Options drop-down list, choose 'View accreditation' to open the 'Employer accreditation certificate' page. 5. Choose 'Request a job check' and choose the type of job check from the drop-down list to start your application. 6. Complete and submit the online form. You will need to pay a fee. Paying for AEWV accreditation and job checks 7. We process your application — if we need more information, we email you to request it. 8. We email you with our decision. Note Check our wait times page to find up-to-date information on how long we are taking to process applications. Employer accreditation, job check and AEWV wait times Include multiple jobs in a job check application You can include multiple jobs in one job check application as long as: the job details are the same ⁠— for example, they are all under the same ANZSCO or NOL code, and have the same occupation, pay, location and minimum requirements they are covered by the same advertising and employment agreement. If the location, job title, or terms of work are different then you need a separate job check for each job. Share your application In Immigration Online, employers can share information about their organisation, accreditations, job checks and migrant workers. This means licensed immigration advisers and other users can work on job checks together. You can create a network in your Immigration Online dashboard. To add other people to your network, they need to provide you with their sharing ID and email address. Sharing an online visa application Enter the correct location for the job In your job check application, you must: enter the location where most of the work will be based or take place if the work will be based in more than 1 location, you must enter each location state if some travel is required. If the job requires some occasional travel or is based in 1 region but with some travel, you only need to list the location the person will be based in. If multiple jobs are included in your job check, the location details must be the same for each job. Example You are applying for a job check for a truck driver that will be based in Hamilton. The job requires daily travel across the Waikato, but the worker will return to the Hamilton depot each night. You can list the location as Hamilton in your job check application. You are applying for a job check for an engineer job. The worker will spend 6 months of the year in New Plymouth and 6 months in Dunedin. You must advertise in both locations and list both locations in your job check application. If we ask you for more information If we ask you for further information, we send you a link to upload up to 5 additional documents. If you have more than 5 documents to upload, follow the instructions in our Enhanced Immigration Online User Guide in section '4.4 Correspondence'. Enhancements to Immigration Online — A guide for users (pdf, 4.3 MB) There is a maximum file size limit of 10 MB each file. If you have issues uploading a document, you can try: reducing the file size of the document, or splitting the document into multiple smaller documents. If your application is declined If we decline your job check application, you can: ask us to reconsider our decision, or submit a new application. If your employer accreditation or job check application is declined

### Step 5: Apply online
If you have everything you need to apply for a job check, start your application.
1. Log into Immigration Online using RealMe®
Log in using RealMe®
2. Go to the 'Employ migrants' tab or choose the 'Employ migrants' button.
3. Go to the 'My granted accreditations' section and find the organisation in the list.
4. In the Options drop-down list, choose 'View accreditation' to open the 'Employer accreditation certificate' page.
5. Choose 'Request a job check' and choose the type of job check from the drop-down list to start your application.
6. Complete and submit the online form. You will need to pay a fee.
Paying for AEWV accreditation and job checks
7. We process your application — if we need more information, we email you to request it.
8. We email you with our decision.
Check our wait times page to find up-to-date information on how long we are taking to process applications.
Employer accreditation, job check and AEWV wait times

#### Include multiple jobs in a job check application
You can include multiple jobs in one job check application as long as:
- the job details are the same ⁠— for example, they are all under the same ANZSCO or NOL code, and have the same occupation, pay, location and minimum requirements
- they are covered by the same advertising and employment agreement.
If the location, job title, or terms of work are different then you need a separate job check for each job.

#### Share your application
In Immigration Online, employers can share information about their organisation, accreditations, job checks and migrant workers.
This means licensed immigration advisers and other users can work on job checks together.
You can create a network in your Immigration Online dashboard. To add other people to your network, they need to provide you with their sharing ID and email address.
Sharing an online visa application

#### Enter the correct location for the job
In your job check application, you must:
- enter the location where most of the work will be based or take place if the work will be based in more than 1 location, you must enter each location state if some travel is required.
- enter the location where most of the work will be based or take place
- if the work will be based in more than 1 location, you must enter each location state if some travel is required.
If the job requires some occasional travel or is based in 1 region but with some travel, you only need to list the location the person will be based in.
If multiple jobs are included in your job check, the location details must be the same for each job.
- You are applying for a job check for a truck driver that will be based in Hamilton. The job requires daily travel across the Waikato, but the worker will return to the Hamilton depot each night. You can list the location as Hamilton in your job check application.
- You are applying for a job check for an engineer job. The worker will spend 6 months of the year in New Plymouth and 6 months in Dunedin. You must advertise in both locations and list both locations in your job check application.

#### If we ask you for more information
If we ask you for further information, we send you a link to upload up to 5 additional documents.
If you have more than 5 documents to upload, follow the instructions in our Enhanced Immigration Online User Guide in section '4.4 Correspondence'.
There is a maximum file size limit of 10 MB each file. If you have issues uploading a document, you can try:
- reducing the file size of the document, or splitting the document into multiple smaller documents.
- reducing the file size of the document, or
- splitting the document into multiple smaller documents.

#### If your application is declined
If we decline your job check application, you can:
- ask us to reconsider our decision, or submit a new application.
- ask us to reconsider our decision, or
- submit a new application.
If your employer accreditation or job check application is declined
- 6 Step 6: Next steps If your job check application is approved, it will expire 6 months from the date it was approved. The next steps to employing migrants on an AEWV are to: log into Immigration Online to view your approved jobs invite a migrant to apply for their AEWV. Recruiting and supporting a migrant to apply for an AEWV: process steps
If your job check application is approved, it will expire 6 months from the date it was approved.
The next steps to employing migrants on an AEWV are to:
- log into Immigration Online to view your approved jobs
- invite a migrant to apply for their AEWV.
Recruiting and supporting a migrant to apply for an AEWV: process steps
Our employer helpline is open between 08:00 and 18:00, Monday to Friday.
Freephone from New Zealand landlines: 0508 558 855
- Advertising the job before your job check Before you can apply for an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) job check, you must advertise the job for at least 2 weeks, unless the job is exempt.

## Advertising the job before your job check
Before you can apply for an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) job check, you must advertise the job for at least 2 weeks, unless the job is exempt.
- Engaging with Work and Income before you apply for a job check If you want to hire a migrant for a job that is ANZSCO skill level 4 or 5, you must engage in good faith with Work and Income to check if there are any New Zealanders workers or residents who can do the job.

## Engaging with Work and Income before you apply for a job check
If you want to hire a migrant for a job that is ANZSCO skill level 4 or 5, you must engage in good faith with Work and Income to check if there are any New Zealanders workers or residents who can do the job.
- How we calculate pay rates for the AEWV When you apply for a job check we can look at the information you supply in the application form to calculate the worker's hourly rate (pay rate).

## How we calculate pay rates for the AEWV
When you apply for a job check we can look at the information you supply in the application form to calculate the worker's hourly rate (pay rate).
- Applying for a job check: employer checklist This checklist is to help accredited employers who need to complete a job check before hiring migrant workers on the regular Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV), Global Workforce Seasonal Visa (GWSV) and Peak Seasonal Visa (PSV)

## Applying for a job check: employer checklist
This checklist is to help accredited employers who need to complete a job check before hiring migrant workers on the regular Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV), Global Workforce Seasonal Visa (GWSV) and Peak Seasonal Visa (PSV)
